recap: Write numbers as products of prime factors, then use shared primes (lower powers) for HCF and all primes (higher powers) for LCM.

questions.question: Write 36 as a product of its prime factors.

steps: Written in index form: 2 squared x 3 squared.
48 = 2 to the power 4 x 3. 36 = 2 squared x 3 squared.
Shared primes with lower powers: 2 squared and 3 to the power 1.
All primes with highest powers: 2 squared, 3 squared, 5.

questions.question: Bus A leaves the station every 12 minutes. Bus B leaves every 18 minutes. They both leave at 9:00 am. At what time do they next leave at the same time?

steps: Find LCM of 12 and 18. 12 = 2 squared x 3. 18 = 2 x 3 squared.
They next leave together 36 minutes after 9:00 am = 9:36 am.

questions.question: Two numbers have an HCF of 6 and an LCM of 180. One of the numbers is 12. Find the other number. Show your working.

steps: Use the rule: HCF x LCM = product of the two numbers.
Check: 12 = 2 squared x 3. 90 = 2 x 3 squared x 5. HCF = 2 x 3 = 6. LCM = 2 squared x 3 squared x 5 = 180. Both check out.

summary: Prime factorisation: divide repeatedly by the smallest prime factor until you reach 1, then write as a product of primes in index form.
HCF: multiply the shared prime factors using the lower power of each.
LCM: multiply all prime factors that appear, using the higher power of each.
HCF x LCM = product of the two numbers — useful shortcut for checking answers.
